summary: The format inventory gains `json-document` — a file member whose whole artifact is one JSON object, the mechanic both producer kinds need

new.description: The format's acceptance, proven over a custom kind fixture rather than a built-in (no built-in declares it until PLUGIN-MANIFEST-KIND lands): a declared `json-document` kind reads its artifact's top-level keys as the member's fields, takes its identity from the declared identity field, and emit renders the member back byte-identically. The read↔write round-trip and double-emit determinism are the two properties (`specs/model/pipeline.md`, 'Emit').

edit.description: `Format` (the enum at 503, re-cited at 63e5b97 — 9f22de2 moved this file) carries exactly one variant today, and its doc (496-501) calls `YamlFrontmatter` 'the first and only harvested entry' (498-499). `JsonDocument` is the second, and that sentence is rewritten to state what stays true rather than left narrating a retired count. `format_from_label` (858-863) gains the `"json-document"` label; an unknown label stays a load error. Identity reuses `UnitShape::NamedField { field }` (the third variant, 526-530) — a JSON document's identity is read from a declared top-level key, not the filename stem — so its doc, which says 'frontmatter field' at both 523-525 and 528, re-words to the fact that survives both formats: the identity is read from a declared field, whichever format carries it. No new unit shape. The `Format::YamlFrontmatter` doc-link at 73 rides along if its sentence stops being true.

**The dispatch that makes `format` load-bearing, and the heart of this entry.** Re-verified at 63e5b97: nothing in the tree branches on `Format` — `rg 'Format::YamlFrontmatter'` finds only four constructions (`src/builtin_kind.rs:73/120/142/175`), the label parse (`src/kind.rs:860`), two doc-links (73/499), and one test assert (1576). Today's adapter dispatch is the 3-arm match at 1160 (unmoved by the window) over `(content, collection_address, governs)`: a fields-only kind with a collection address reads through `manifest_units`, a nested-file kind through `discover_nested_file`, and **every other file kind falls through to `read_file_unit` unconditionally** — the frontmatter/layout adapter, whatever the kind's declared format says. `json-document` is the first format that makes the declared fact decide anything, so a file kind declaring it must route to the JSON adapter here. Keep it one dispatch: a per-call-site match on format is the second-implementation residue class (`specs/process/engineering.md`, 'One job, one home'). The comment at 1155-1159, which narrates the two-way split as the whole story, moves with the code.

The one home for JSON mechanics: the module is already 'the generic JSON-manifest adapter — `frontmatter.rs`'s peer for structured config', with a read face (`read` at 163, `read_kind` at 251 — re-cited at 63e5b97) and a canonical whole-manifest write face (`write_manifest`, 288). A second JSON reader beside it is the duplicate-surface disease — extend this module. The new case is a **container-as-member** read: no collection address, so every top-level key is the member's own field rather than opaque residue, and identity comes from the declared identity field. The write face renders one member's fields back as the whole document, LF-terminated (`specs/model/pipeline.md`: line endings are layout). Both faces stay pure, holding the header's idempotence keystone.

`export type Format = "yaml-frontmatter"` (17, unmoved) becomes the two-label union. The `format` field's doc (144-145, 'omitted for a frontmatterless kind' — re-cited at 63e5b97) narrates the retired one-format world and re-words. `tsc` rejects an unknown label at the keystroke, the same closed vocabulary the engine's load guard holds.

`KindFactRow.format` is string-typed (`sdk/src/generated/KindFactRow.ts`), so the new label round-trips with no row-shape change and `tests/seam_bindings_current.rs` stays green untouched. If it goes red the shape moved — bless the bindings, never hand-edit `sdk/src/generated/`.

tests.asserts: a `json-document` kind's artifact reads as one member — top-level keys its fields, identity from the declared field — and re-emits byte-identically; double-emit is stable; an unknown format label is a load error, never a skip

acceptance: `json-document` is the format inventory's second entry, in the closed vocabulary on both sides of the seam (Rust `Format`, SDK `Format`), implemented once and generically in the engine (`specs/model/representation.md`, 'kind': a kind is data, its extractor composed from that data). A kind's declared format now decides which adapter reads it, at the one dispatch — the fact stops being decorative. The JSON mechanics live in `src/json_manifest.rs`, extended, not re-implemented beside it. `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Derived from decision 0031 (16ce347), which names it: plugin-manifest is 'JSON document format (a deliberate format-inventory addition — format mechanics are engine code)'. Ships ahead of its two consumers because one entry is one gate-sized commit; the fixture-only proof is the shape the nested-file locus took before `supporting-doc` counted it. Blocker INSTALLED-PLUGIN-KIND shipped (9f22de2); gate released. scoped at e81c758; every cite above re-derived on disk at 63e5b97.

summary: `plugin-manifest` ships: `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` as a typed file member, identity from `name`, its default contract the `claude plugin validate --strict` profile

new.description: The kind's acceptance: a `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` fixture surfaces one member with identity from `name`; a manifest missing `name` is a finding at the declared severity; an unrecognized top-level field is the `--strict` case — the runtime ignores it and loads, `--strict` treats it as an error — so the clause's guidance must say which world it holds (`specs/builtins.md`, 'Default contracts': where the runtime diverges from the spec, the clause's guidance says so).

edit.description: `claude_code_plugin_manifest()` joins `all_kinds()` (315, re-cited at 63e5b97 — 9f22de2 moved it): `format: Some(Format::JsonDocument)`, `unit_shape: NamedField { field: "name" }`, `governs { root: ".claude-plugin", glob: "plugin.json" }`, and no collection address — it owns its file. A distinct glob from every sibling's, so `governs_collision_diagnostics` (`src/main.rs:1801`) is satisfied by construction. Cite `code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference` with the retrieval date at encode time.
Generated data, never hand-edited (`src/builtin_lock.rs:17-19`) — gains the kind row and its default-contract clause rows. Regenerate per the file's header.
Module header and tests, as a7a8cc1 moved them for `supporting-doc`.

The `PluginManifest` type, the `plugin-manifest` kind value, and `pluginManifestDefaultContract` — modelled on `skillDefaultContract`, which already holds this exact stance and states it in its own header. **Fetch the facts fresh at encode time and cite per clause** (`specs/builtins.md`, 'The clauses live in code': cited and dated, carried as data). Verified this tick against `code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference` (retrieved 2026-07-16) and re-verifiable at pick-up: `name` is the ONE required field — 'Unique identifier (kebab-case, no spaces)' — and when a marketplace entry lists the plugin under a different name, the marketplace entry's name is what `enabledPlugins` keys. The metadata fields (`$schema`, `displayName`, `version`, `description`, …) and the component path fields (`skills`, `commands`, `agents` as `string|array`; `hooks` as `string|array|object`) are optional and typed — a wrong-typed field ('a string instead of an array is a load error, and `claude plugin validate` reports it as one') is decidable and fails everywhere, so it is clause territory. An unrecognized top-level field is `--strict`'s warning-as-error: the runtime deliberately ignores it ('Claude Code ignores top-level fields it does not recognize'), and `--strict` is the portable bar decision 0031 ruled — exactly the runtime-diverges case the guidance channel carries. `version` in `plugin.json` beats the marketplace entry's; per-field precedence is clause-and-cite territory here, never a spec enumeration (0031). What stays out: anything undecidable, per the same section.
The provider face exports the kind value, its type, and its default contract.
The kind's facts and its default contract's clause set — each clause's predicate, severity, and the presence of its cite.
`MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM` imports and `expect`-binds the kind, or its rows never reach the lock and the byte-compare fails.

The row-count-exact kind-fact walk: the new row and its `json-document` format label. `the_embedded_lock_kind_facts_match_todays_hand_written_kinds` asserts `declarations.kinds.len()` — **9 at 63e5b97** (2093; 9f22de2 moved it 8 → 9), so it moves to 10 here. The engine roster and `specs/builtins.md`'s 'Ten kinds ship' count different sets and are not each other's check: `supporting-doc` ships without joining that enumeration (as `requirement` does), so the walk's number is always the corpus's plus one. Read the assert, never the arithmetic.

tests.asserts: a `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` reads as one member with identity from `name`; a missing `name` and a wrong-typed component-path field are findings; the `--strict` unrecognized-field clause fires with guidance naming the runtime divergence

acceptance: `plugin-manifest` ships as `specs/builtins.md`'s roster declares it — the pack's identity at `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, a JSON document, identity from `name`, the one required field, kebab-case — with a default contract mirroring the strictest documented profile (`claude plugin validate --strict`), every clause decidable, severity-declared, guided, cited and dated. This repo authors no `.claude-plugin/` today, so the kind counts zero members here and that is honest, not a gap (the `supporting-doc (0)` precedent). `temper check .` exits 0; `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Derived from decision 0031 (16ce347), the producer half. Its warrant is not speculative demand: `temper bundle` already writes this exact file ungated (`src/bundle.rs:185`), so the product emits a format it refuses to check — temper's own bar failing in temper. Serialized behind PLUGIN-JSON-DOCUMENT-FORMAT (needs the format) and ahead of MARKETPLACE-KIND (both edit `builtin_kind.rs`, `builtins.ts`, `builtin_lock.toml`, and the two lock tests). scoped at e81c758; re-cited 63e5b97.

summary: `marketplace` ships: `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` as a typed file member — identity from `name` against the reserved-names deny list, `owner.name` required, each entry's `source` union

new.description: The kind's acceptance: a `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` fixture surfaces one member with identity from `name`; a reserved name is a finding; a missing `owner.name` is a finding; a `plugins[]` entry missing `name` or `source` is a finding; each documented `source` form resolves and an undocumented one is a finding.

edit.description: `claude_code_marketplace()` joins `all_kinds()` (315, re-cited at 63e5b97 — 9f22de2 moved it): `format: Some(Format::JsonDocument)`, `unit_shape: NamedField { field: "name" }`, `governs { root: ".claude-plugin", glob: "marketplace.json" }` — a distinct glob from `plugin-manifest`'s, so the two file kinds never contend. Cite `code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces` with the retrieval date at encode time.
Generated data, never hand-edited — the kind row plus its clause rows. Regenerate per the file's header.
Module header and tests.

The `Marketplace` type, the kind value, and `marketplaceDefaultContract`. **Re-fetch and cite per clause at encode time.** Verified this tick against `code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces` (retrieved 2026-07-16), every fact below decidable: `name` is kebab-case and checked against a documented **reserved-names deny list** — `claude-code-marketplace`, `claude-code-plugins`, `claude-plugins-official`, `claude-plugins-community`, `claude-community`, `anthropic-marketplace`, `anthropic-plugins`, `agent-skills`, `anthropic-agent-skills`, `knowledge-work-plugins`, `life-sciences`, `claude-for-legal`, `claude-for-financial-services`, `financial-services-plugins`, `first-party-plugins`, `healthcare` — re-read the list at encode time rather than trusting this transcription, since the page itself records the list growing (before v2.1.205, `first-party-plugins` and `healthcare` were not reserved). The honest bound: the docs ALSO block names that 'impersonate official marketplaces' (`official-claude-plugins`, `anthropic-plugins-v2`), and impersonation is semantic judgment — so the enumerated deny list is the decidable subset and the impersonation rule is guidance, never a clause (intent.md, invariant 2). `owner.name` required, `owner.email` optional. Each `plugins[]` entry requires `name` plus `source`, the documented union: a relative-path string (must start with `./`, resolved relative to the marketplace root, NOT `.claude-plugin/`), or an object — `github` (`repo`, `ref?`, `sha?`), `url` (`url`, `ref?`, `sha?`), `git-subdir` (`url`, `path`, `ref?`, `sha?`), `npm` (`package`, `version?`, `registry?`). Where both `ref` and `sha` are set on a git-based source, `sha` is the effective pin; a marketplace entry's `defaultEnabled` beats `plugin.json`'s. Per-field precedence is clause-and-cite territory here (decision 0031).

The provider face exports the kind value, its type, and its default contract. With this entry `specs/builtins.md`'s roster is filled: 'Ten kinds ship. Seven are file members' — seven file kinds (skill, command, agent, rule, memory, plugin-manifest, marketplace) plus three registration kinds (hook, mcp-server, installed-plugin). The engine's `all_kinds()` holds **eleven** at that point, because `supporting-doc` ships outside the enumeration; the two numbers are both right and neither checks the other. Verify on disk.
The kind's facts and its default contract's clause set, the deny-list clause included.
`MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM` imports and `expect`-binds the kind.

The row-count-exact kind-fact walk: the `kinds.len()` assert (2093 at 63e5b97) moves to **eleven**, not ten — `supporting-doc` ships without joining `specs/builtins.md`'s enumeration (as `requirement` does), so the engine roster runs one above the corpus's ten. Read the assert on disk at pick-up; do not trust this number sight-unseen.

tests.asserts: a `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` reads as one member with identity from `name`; a reserved name, a missing `owner.name`, and a `plugins[]` entry missing `name` or `source` are each findings; every documented `source` form resolves and an undocumented one is a finding

acceptance: `marketplace` ships as `specs/builtins.md`'s roster declares it, completing the ten-kind roster decision 0031 ratified — the seven file kinds and three registration kinds that page enumerates, `supporting-doc` shipping beside them outside the count. `temper check .` exits 0. Its default contract holds the strictest documented profile with every clause decidable, severity-declared, guided, cited and dated; the impersonation rule ships as guidance, never a clause. `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Derived from decision 0031 (16ce347), the producer half's second kind. Same warrant as its sibling: `temper bundle` writes this file ungated today (`src/bundle.rs:191`). Serialized behind PLUGIN-MANIFEST-KIND — both edit `builtin_kind.rs`, `builtins.ts`, `claude-code.ts`, `builtin_lock.toml`, `builtin_lock.rs`, and the two lock tests. scoped at e81c758; cites re-derived 63e5b97, and the stale 'ten kind rows' arithmetic this entry carried corrected against the assert on disk.

summary: `temper bundle`'s two hard-coded `serde_json` manifest writers reconcile onto the shipped kinds — emit through the kind system, one home

edit.description: `run()` (178) writes `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (185) and `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (191) through `write_json` over the hand-built `plugin_manifest()` and `marketplace_manifest()` values — all cites re-verified by reading at e81c758. Both are a second implementation of a job the kind system owns once `plugin-manifest` and `marketplace` ship: the manifests become members of their kinds, rendered by the kind's format, so `bundle` stops hand-spelling a shape the engine can now type and gate. This is the consolidation decision 0031 names ('bundle's hard-coded writers reconcile onto the kinds — emit through the kind system, one home; the entry names the unification'), and `specs/process/engineering.md` makes it the entry rather than scope creep — name the unification in the commit body. NOT in scope: the `SessionStart` hook's `hooks/hooks.json` (`hooks_manifest()`) and the skill's `SKILL.md` — `hooks.json` is a plugin-cache artifact of neither kind, and widening to it is the creep the same page forbids. If any part of either manifest's shape cannot route through a kind, say so plainly rather than leaving a silent hand-rolled remainder.

The bundle's existing acceptance. Its assertions over both manifests are the regression net that makes this a true refactor: the emitted bytes should not move, and if they do, that is a behavior change to state out loud in the commit body, never a snapshot to bless quietly.

tests.asserts: `bundle` emits both `.claude-plugin/` manifests through their kinds' formats, byte-identically to the pre-refactor output, and the emitted manifests pass their own kinds' default contracts — the product checks what it writes

acceptance: The two manifest writers have one home: `bundle`'s output is its members rendered by their kinds' formats, not hand-built `serde_json` beside a kind that types the same shape (`specs/process/engineering.md`, 'One job, one home': a job the tree already performs is extended where it lives, never reimplemented beside itself). The bundle temper publishes passes the contract temper ships for it, closing the gap decision 0031 named — the product wrote files it refused to check. `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: Derived from decision 0031 (16ce347), the third derivation item. Blocked until both kinds exist — there is nothing to reconcile onto before then. Distinct from making this repo a plugin-author corpus (the `(plugin-author-dogfood)` fork): `bundle` still assembles the tree it emits into an output dir, and no `.claude-plugin/` is committed here (verified absent at e81c758). scoped at e81c758.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. All cites re-derived at 53519b9, which moved this file again. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (65) sits under a doc (61-64) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (627) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (654), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 525-526 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] as the format documents') and 624-625 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 649-652 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1356, re-derived at 53519b9 from the 1402 it was last scoped at — 1b9a6af lifted this file's private harness helpers into `tests/common` and rewired it, moving the region) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding (1386-1388) — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1357-1361) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1359) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 63e5b97 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics (b8396d4, the 0028 errata, touches no import mechanics; inbox empty). INSTALLED-PLUGIN-KIND edited `src/graph.rs` only at 690+ (`dead_registration`'s enablement arm): every cite above is disjoint and unmoved, verified this tick. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` still reads 5 at 65 under a cite claiming five. Stays parked: the constant may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified 63e5b97.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 63e5b97, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. Channel 3's publish is the release act; BUNDLE-EMIT-THROUGH-KINDS is the orthogonal question of how those bytes are produced. scoped at b676656.
